.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/017-hook-test-sandbox-fix/research/prompts/iteration-001.md:97:- New aggregate: Direct smoke = 5 PASS / 0 FAIL; Live CLI = 5 BLOCKED_BY_TEST_SANDBOX (deferred to operator-run-outside-sandbox)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/028-documentation-alignment-readme-fill-in/implementation-summary.md:47:Five doc-quality drift items under `system-spec-kit/` are now aligned with sk-doc canonical templates, and the parallel `operator_runbook` and `manual_testing_playbook` surfaces under `skill_advisor/` are merged into one canonical playbook. Future authors who copy these folders as patterns now inherit a clean precedent rather than amplifying the drift.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/028-documentation-alignment-readme-fill-in/implementation-summary.md:51:You can now treat every file under `references/multi-ai-council/` as a sk-doc-compliant reference. All six docs (`command-wiring`, `convergence-signals`, `folder-layout`, `output-schema`, `seat-diversity-patterns`, `state-format`) carry YAML frontmatter with `contextType: "reference"`, a 1-2 sentence intro, a horizontal rule divider, and numbered uppercase H2 sections starting with `## 1. OVERVIEW`. The validator now passes all six as `reference` type with zero issues.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/028-documentation-alignment-readme-fill-in/implementation-summary.md:67:`mcp_server/skill_advisor/operator_runbook/` is gone. Its 42 scenarios across nine subsystem categories (NC, CL, CP, OP, AU, AI, LC, SC, PC) now live under `manual_testing_playbook/` as the canonical surface. Each per-feature file passes the `playbook_feature` validator with the four required sections (`overview`, `scenario_contract`, `test_execution`, `source_metadata`). The four former `SAD-NNN` scenarios are absorbed into their NC/CL counterparts with explicit "Absorbed from former SAD-NNN" notes; a §18 LEGACY ID CROSS-REFERENCE in the entry-point file maps the deprecated IDs to their new homes. The entry-point document follows the sk-doc playbook template across §1-17 with per-category summary tables and a recommended wave plan.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/028-documentation-alignment-readme-fill-in/implementation-summary.md:75:Phase 0 scaffolded the Level 3 packet (`spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `resource-map.md`) directly via the `create.sh --phase` workflow plus manual authoring, since `--phase` mode produces Level 1 boilerplate that needed Level 3 content. Wave A and Wave B work-blocks landed via direct file edits after the initial parallel `cli-codex` dispatches stalled in network-wait (the project's documented unreliability pattern for parallel codex shells). Wave C, the operator-runbook merge, used a hybrid path: a single `cli-codex` shell with full-auto sandbox kicked off but ultimately stalled at zero CPU; a follow-up bash + sed transformation completed the heading rename across 41 files while git restore recovered the 110-LOC NC-006 file that the partial codex run had emptied. The merged entry-point document was completed by the prior codex shell before the kill, and the validation pass was a final `validate_document.py` sweep across every modified or created file.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/028-documentation-alignment-readme-fill-in/implementation-summary.md:85:| Preserve all 43 operator scenarios; absorb 4 SAD overlaps into NC/CL counterparts (ADR D-1) | Trimming would have lost subsystem coverage that took years to build; absorption resolved the parallel-folder duplication without the rewrite cost |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/003-system-bug-improvement-research/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:20:| F-001-A1-04 | P2 | `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/skill_advisor/lib/freshness/generation.ts:123-127` | `publishSkillGraphGeneration()` releases the generation lock before calling `invalidateSkillGraphCaches()`. Another publisher can write generation N+1 and invalidate it before generation N's invalidation fan-out runs; `cache-invalidation.ts:31-37` then overwrites `lastInvalidation` and notifies listeners with an older generation after the newer one. | Either emit invalidation while still holding the generation publication lock, or make invalidation listeners/state monotonic by ignoring events whose `generation` is older than the last observed generation. |

For canonical spec docs, yes: `handleMemorySave()` validates and calls `indexMemoryFile()` (`memory-save.ts:3060`), `createMemoryRecord()` persists to `memory_index`/vector or deferred index plus BM25 (`save/create-record.ts:310`), and successful responses run post-mutation invalidation (`save/response-builder.ts:552`). `memory_index_scan()` also indexes discovered spec docs and graph metadata (`memory-index.ts:263`) and invalidates caches after index/update/stale-delete (`memory-index.ts:647`).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:15:    next_safe_action: "Run validate.sh --strict; dispatch Tier 2a wave"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:402:## ADR-005: Bounded-wave parallel cli-codex execution
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:416:Total scope is 25-35 cli-codex dispatches across Tier 2 sub-phases. Running all in parallel maxes API throughput and creates coordination/recovery problems if any dispatch produces inconsistent output. Running serially would take 90-200 min.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:428:**We chose**: Bounded wave execution. Each wave dispatches 6-12 cli-codex agents in parallel. Waves: 2a (8 low-effort) → verify → 2b (12 medium-effort) → verify → 2c (5 high-effort, single-scope) → verify.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:438:| **Bounded waves (chosen)** | Failure radius small; verification between waves; matches user's "max parallel" directive | Wall time 2-4 hours instead of 90 min | 9/10 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:439:| Full parallelism (all 25 simultaneously) | Fastest wall time | Failure recovery is chaos; API throttling likely | 4/10 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:440:| Serial dispatch | Lowest risk | 90-200 min wall time; doesn't honor "max parallel" directive | 3/10 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:442:**Why this one**: Bounded parallelism balances throughput with recovery sanity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:449:- Failed dispatches caught + re-dispatched within a wave without blocking subsequent waves
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:451:- User's "max parallel" directive honored within sane bounds
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:454:- Wall time ~2-4 hours instead of theoretical ~90 min. Mitigation: verification between waves catches failures early.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:460:| API throttling at 12 concurrent dispatches | M | Drop to 6-8 per wave if rate limits hit |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:461:| Verification between waves is too cursory | M | Full `validate_document.py` per modified root + spot-check 1 file per dispatch |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:470:| 2 | **Beyond Local Maxima?** | PASS | Considered full-parallel + serial before bounded |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:471:| 3 | **Sufficient?** | PASS | Bounded waves balance throughput with recovery |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:472:| 4 | **Fits Goal?** | PASS | Honors "max parallel" directive within sane bounds |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:482:- Tier 2 broken into 3 waves (2a/2b/2c) per plan.md §4
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:483:- Verify gate between waves (run validators + spot-check)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:485:**How to roll back**: Halt the in-flight wave (`pkill -f "codex.*model.*gpt-5.5"`); per-file revert via `git restore`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:538:- No branch-merge conflicts with parallel in-flight work
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/decision-record.md:547:| Conflicting parallel work on main | M | This packet's modified files are largely orthogonal to other in-flight work |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/spec.md:53:**Critical Dependencies**: sk-doc skill (canonical format definitions); sk-improve-prompt (RCAF prompt generation); cli-codex CLI (parallel gpt-5.5 high fast content rewrites).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/spec.md:74:The user spotted format drift in two cited manual_testing_playbook examples (`system-spec-kit/mcp_server/skill_advisor/` and `.../code_graph/`). Seven parallel cli-codex gpt-5.5 high fast audits revealed a far wider pattern: most playbooks, catalogs, and references across the tree drift from the canonical sk-doc shape, including the canonical `system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/` itself (320/321 per-feature files use `## 4. REFERENCES` instead of `## 4. SOURCE FILES`). On top of that, the templates folder carries a legacy `templates/sharded/`, a hyphenated `templates/stress-test/` missing its README, and a custom `templates/changelog/` that drifts from sk-doc shape.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/spec.md:184:- **NFR-P01**: Tier 2 dispatch wave wall time ≤45 minutes per wave (8-12 parallel cli-codex agents per wave)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/spec.md:192:- **NFR-R01**: Failed cli-codex dispatches are diagnosed + re-dispatched within the wave; do not block subsequent waves

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/004-fix-deep-research-findings/009-fix-test-reliability/spec.md:67:Six tests in the stress-test and unit-test surface have reliability defects that make them brittle on non-developer machines, in CI, or under repeated runs. One test hard-codes the original developer's local checkout path. One asserts absolute latency budgets that can fail on slower hardware. One uses real-timer sleeps with elapsed-time assertions, slowing the suite and producing flakes. Two tests mutate process env vars (`SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_PLUGIN_DISABLED`, `SPECKIT_MMR`) without restoration, leaking state across the run. One test uses a repo-local temp fixture root that collides between parallel runs and survives crashes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/002-audit/001-dependency-security-supply-chain-audit/research/prompts/iteration-015.md:40:- If a CRITICAL direct IOC is found, finish the iteration output and mark the convergence verdict COMPROMISE-CONFIRMED.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/plan.md:16:    next_safe_action: "Run validate.sh --strict; dispatch Tier 2a wave"

Each Tier-2 sub-phase fans out to parallel cli-codex gpt-5.5 high fast agents. Tier 1 is sequential foundation. Tier 3 is mostly deterministic git operations. Tier 4 is sequential validation. Total estimated effort: 25-35 cli-codex dispatches; ~2-4 hours wall time with full parallelism; ~150-300 files modified.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/plan.md:62:- [x] Audit complete via 7 parallel cli-codex gpt-5.5 high fast agents (audit-findings.md)

Each wave verifies before the next starts.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/spec.md:50:After this session's wave of remediation work (013 research → 031-044 implementation + audits + finalization), the 026-graph-and-context-optimization wrapper packet is approximately release-ready. But "approximately" is not "verified." Before declaring release-ready, run a structured 10-angle audit program where each angle scrutinizes a distinct release-critical dimension.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/spec.md:52:This phase parent organizes 10 independent deep-review audits, each scoped to one of the 10 release-readiness angles. Children are independent — they can run in parallel (with concurrency=3 to honor cli-codex throttling). Each child produces its own `review-report.md` with severity-weighted P0/P1/P2 findings + remediation tickets.

Architectural decisions D-001 through D-006 resolved in `decision-record.md`. Tier 1 foundation work is ~95% complete with strict validation as the remaining gate.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/implementation-summary.md:63:Pre-implementation placeholder. Post-Tier-4 narrative will document: bounded-wave execution model (3 waves: 2a/2b/2c) with verification between waves, parallel cli-codex gpt-5.5 high fast dispatches per surface, deterministic git operations for renames, and sequential validation. The cli-codex stdin gotcha (codex hangs waiting for stdin when prompt passed as positional arg) was discovered + mitigated by piping prompts via `< /tmp/prompt.txt -` syntax.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/021-sk-doc-conformance-template-sweep/implementation-summary.md:77:| D-005 | Bounded-wave parallel cli-codex execution (3 waves: 2a/2b/2c) | Accepted |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/004-followup-post-program/003-post-program-quality-pass/006-readme-cascade-refresh/target-list.md:51:| 037/004 | Fixed README/reference structure drift and deferred raw matrix prompt-template rewrites |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:3:description: "Release-readiness deep-review report for deep-loop convergence, JSONL state, lineage, validation, prompt-pack, and reducer integrity."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:7:  - "convergence detection review"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:18:The deep-loop stack has several solid pieces: lineage reducers restrict review mode to `new`, `resume`, and `restart`; malformed JSONL fails closed by default; graph `STOP_BLOCKED` is wired into the review blocked-stop path; current prompt templates use flat state-path variables that the prompt-pack renderer can substitute; and the research auto YAML has the newer audited executor wrappers.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:34:**Evidence:** The review convergence reference calls max iterations a hard stop: "Hard stops are evaluated first and override all other signals" and `len(iterations) >= config.maxIterations` returns `STOP` with `maxIterationsReached` `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:112` and `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:119`. It also names `maxIterations` a "Hard iteration ceiling" `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:715`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:36:The deep-review YAML weakens that guarantee. It says hard stops are "still subject to step 5 legal-stop gates" `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:445`, including the `max_iterations_reached` branch `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:446`. If any gate fails, the STOP candidate becomes `decision = "BLOCKED"` `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:476`, and the convergence handler continues rather than synthesizing `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:551`. The confirm workflow carries the same shape: max iterations promotes a STOP candidate `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:459`, any failing gate turns it into BLOCKED `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:489`, and blocked handling continues `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:564`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:38:Research has the same class of bug. The research reference says max iterations returns `STOP` with `maxIterationsReached` `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:979`, but the auto YAML applies quality guard override to any `decision == "STOP"` `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:451`; a failed guard sets `decision = "CONTINUE"` `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:453`. The confirm workflow also sets the composite reason to legacy `composite_converged` and uses the same guardable STOP shape `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:455`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:42:**Concrete fix:** Split terminal hard caps from legal convergence. `maxIterationsReached`, `userPaused`, `manualStop`, and unrecoverable `error` should bypass legal-stop/quality gate vetoes. They may record failed gates as terminal evidence, but they must still emit `synthesis_complete` with `stopReason:"maxIterationsReached"` and exit to synthesis. Add regression fixtures for review and research where the cap is reached with missing coverage and active blockers.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:62:**Evidence:** The review state-format contract requires iteration records to include `mode`, `run`, `status`, `focus`, `dimensions`, `filesReviewed`, `findingsCount`, `findingsSummary`, `findingsNew`, `newFindingsRatio`, `sessionId`, `generation`, `lineageMode`, `timestamp`, and `durationMs` `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:195`. It also declares `filesReviewed` as `string[]` `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:203`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:66:**Impact:** A review iteration can pass post-dispatch validation while lacking lineage fields, status, focus, timestamp, duration, and an array-valued `filesReviewed`. That weakens replay, synthesis audit, and traceability. It also makes the reducer compensate for malformed records instead of enforcing the append-only state contract at the boundary.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:68:**Concrete fix:** Update the prompt pack examples to match `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md`, including `mode:"review"`, `run`, `status`, `focus`, lineage fields, and array-valued `filesReviewed`. Extend `validateIterationOutputs` with per-mode schema validation or a caller-provided Zod schema, not just required-field presence.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:76:**Impact:** Reviewers can waste time looking for a file that no longer exists at the requested path. The actual graph convergence behavior is still auditable through YAML and code_graph references, but the brief path is stale.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:78:**Concrete fix:** Update release-readiness target lists to point at the live coverage graph handlers or feature catalog paths, or add a compatibility note that graph convergence lives under code_graph rather than `lib/deep-loop`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:103:- Changing convergence thresholds unless empirical stress evidence shows a need.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:104:- Reintroducing deferred lineage modes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:109:2. Update convergence YAML so max-iteration terminal stops bypass legal-stop and quality-gate vetoes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:120:Answer: Partially. Research docs say fewer than 3 iterations skip convergence threshold checks and only max/stuck apply `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:868`, but the research YAML still includes question entropy from iteration one and redistributes weights when rolling/MAD are unavailable `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:421`. Review is more internally consistent: rolling needs 2 iterations, MAD needs 3, dimension coverage can be active immediately, and weights are redistributed `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:455`. The fallback itself is explicit, but the max-cap bug means the terminal path is not safe.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:122:Question: Does `step_graph_convergence` correctly veto premature inline STOP when blockers are present?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:124:Answer: Yes for deep-review's `STOP_BLOCKED` path. The graph step records `CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED` before inline convergence `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:410`, and the combined rule maps `STOP_BLOCKED` to BLOCKED with full blocker detail preserved `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:497`. `step_emit_blocked_stop` then writes `blocked_stop` with `graphBlockerDetail` `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:538`. Caveat: max-iteration STOP can still be entangled with legal-stop blocking, which is P0-001.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:126:Question: Lineage modes `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live; `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred. Does the workflow honor that?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:128:Answer: Mostly yes. Deep-review YAML exposes `auto|resume|restart` as operator intent and explicitly says `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:33`. Resume and restart emit typed lifecycle events with `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `continuedFromRun`, `generation`, and `archivedPath` `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:206` and `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:215`. The review reducer enforces live modes with `LIVE_LINEAGE_MODES = new,resume,restart` and normalizes anything else to null `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:23`. Research loop docs carry the same contract `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:82`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:134:Question: Does prompt-pack rendering correctly substitute variables, especially `{state_paths.*}` and `{config.lineage.*}`?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:136:Answer: Current prompt-pack templates are safe because they use flat variables such as `{state_paths_config}` and `{state_paths_state_log}` `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl:45`. `renderPromptPack` only recognizes flat token names matching `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*` `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts:10`, so a literal `{state_paths.config}` or `{config.lineage.sessionId}` inside a prompt template would not be substituted by this renderer. In YAML command strings, dotted placeholders are a separate workflow interpolation concern, not `renderPromptPack`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/review-report.md:171:- `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/{loop_protocol,convergence,state_format,spec_check_protocol}.md`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/003-system-bug-improvement-research/research/research.md:296:Findings expanded across categories rather than converging to one single subsystem. The strongest convergence was around contract drift: the same failure mode appears in hook rendering, CLI skill templates, YAML workflow flags, schema duplication, spec topology docs, and dist alignment. Concurrency and state publication issues also recur in daemon watcher, code-graph persistence, memory causal edges, and deep-loop fallback records. Direct duplicates were low; adjacent iterations often found different manifestations of the same class rather than the same `file:line` bug. Coverage confidence is high for the selected 20 angles, but not exhaustive for all DB JSON columns, all MCP tool contracts, all external CLI live versions, or all dist/generated artifact policies.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/research/prompts/iteration-001.md:20:- `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/{loop_protocol,convergence,state_format,spec_check_protocol}.md`

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/007-deep-loop-workflow-integrity-audit/research/prompts/iteration-001.md:30:For maintainability: deep-research and deep-review YAMLs share patterns where applicable; convergence-vote logic is testable.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/002-automation-reality-supplemental-research/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:44:Hunter: `deep_loop_graph_convergence` and `deep_loop_graph_upsert` are not test-only. The command markdown says production command execution resolves setup, loads the matching YAML, and executes it step by step (`.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:18-21`, `.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:20-23`). The YAMLs contain live MCP-tool steps for convergence and upsert.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/002-automation-reality-supplemental-research/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:46:Skeptic: Handler tests prove the tools work, but not auto-fire. `deep-loop-graph-query.vitest.ts` imports and calls query/status/upsert handlers directly (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop-graph-query.vitest.ts:30-41`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop-graph-query.vitest.ts:63-138`), and `graph-aware-stop.vitest.ts` imports convergence/upsert directly (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/graph-aware-stop.vitest.ts:29-38`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/graph-aware-stop.vitest.ts:60-114`). Tests do not promote query/status to auto-fire.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/002-automation-reality-supplemental-research/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:48:Referee: `convergence` is `auto` inside the deep-research and deep-review YAML workflows. `upsert` is `half`: the workflow step is real, but it is skipped when the latest iteration lacks `graphEvents`, and iteration prompts explicitly make `graphEvents` optional (`.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl:28-32`, `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl:54-59`). `query` and `status` remain `manual`; exact searches found no command-YAML, bootstrap, watcher, or after-tool path that invokes them automatically beyond the generic requested-tool dispatch path (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:920-1014`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tools/index.ts:86-112`).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/004-fix-deep-research-findings/006-fix-architecture-cleanup-followups/implementation-summary.md:4:description: "Fifteen surgical refactors close architectural findings F-016-D1-01..08, F-017-D2-01..03, and F-018-D3-01..04 from packet 046. Extracts neutral utilities to fix import direction, breaks value-level cycles via shared type modules, removes a dead export, and centralizes vocabulary tuples so schemas/types/guards cannot drift. F-018-D3-04 partial: two critical advisor contracts deduplicated; the broader 60+-tool surface is deferred to a follow-on packet."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/004-fix-deep-research-findings/006-fix-architecture-cleanup-followups/implementation-summary.md:88:| F-018-D3-04 (P2, partial) | `skill_advisor/tools/advisor-contract-keys.ts` (new) + 3 sites | The two most critical advisor tool contracts (`advisor_recommend`, `advisor_validate`) source their JSON Schema property keys and `ALLOWED_PARAMETERS` entries from a single canonical tuple per contract. Drift between the descriptor and the allowlist now surfaces as a TypeScript error at the descriptor site. The broader 60+-tool MCP contract surface remains deferred to a follow-on packet — a clean single-source generation would require introducing `zod-to-json-schema` and refactoring how `validateToolArgs` reads the schemas, which is too large for one sub-phase. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/011-cli-matrix-adapter-runners/research/prompts/iteration-001.md:22:1. Determine adapter target location. Recommended: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/matrix_runners/` (parallel to existing `mcp_server/handlers/`, `mcp_server/lib/`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/006-mcp-tool-schema-governance-audit/spec.md:65:The release-readiness program needs a truth check for the MCP tool schema contract. Every public tool should have one canonical Zod schema, strict mode should reject hallucinated parameters by default, governed ingest should not be bypassable, validation errors should be operator-actionable, and tool counts should not silently inflate through deferred or duplicated registrations.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/001-release-readiness/003-release-readiness-deep-review-audits/006-mcp-tool-schema-governance-audit/spec.md:116:| REQ-001 | Produce a 9-section `review-report.md` with P0/P1/P2 findings. | Report contains executive summary, planning trigger, active finding registry, remediation workstreams, spec seed, plan seed, traceability status, deferred items, and audit appendix. |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/002-automation-reality-supplemental-research/research/research-report.md:56:Deep-loop graph automation is real but narrow. `deep_loop_graph_convergence` auto-fires in deep-research and deep-review YAML workflows before stop voting (`iteration-001.md:40`). `deep_loop_graph_upsert` is half-automated because the workflow step exists but is conditional on emitted `graphEvents` (`iteration-001.md:39`, `iteration-001.md:48`). `deep_loop_graph_query` and `deep_loop_graph_status` are manual MCP surfaces with no YAML, bootstrap, watcher, or after-tool auto-fire path found (`iteration-001.md:38`, `iteration-001.md:41`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/006-research/002-automation-reality-supplemental-research/research/research-report.md:210:Dependency graph: 031 gates the language and source-of-truth cleanup for every later packet. 032 and 033 can run in parallel after 031 because they touch different subsystems. 034 should wait for 032/033 decisions so it does not upgrade or document behavior that those packets retract. 035 should run last, using packet 030's design as the execution matrix.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/007-fix-stress-test-coverage-gap-followup/implementation-summary.md:107:Three sequential cli-codex dispatches generated 9 of the 10 tests. Batch A (gpt-5.5 high) wrote 6 daemon/freshness tests. Batch B (gpt-5.5 high) wrote 3 tests covering anti-stuffing, DF/IDF, and cg-012 deep-loop convergence. Batch C (gpt-5.5 medium) was Gate-3-blocked by codex's session policy at write time, so the sa-037 Python wrapper was authored directly by the orchestrating agent and validated independently. Each batch self-validated by running just its files via vitest before moving on.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/007-fix-stress-test-coverage-gap-followup/implementation-summary.md:134:| Three sequential cli-codex batches, not parallel | Avoids workspace-write contention; each batch self-validates before the next |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/007-fix-stress-test-coverage-gap-followup/implementation-summary.md:170:3. **No flake-soak performed.** Spec REQ-008 was deferred — only one clean run was required for this packet. The user can request a soak run (3+ consecutive `npm run stress` invocations) if any of the new tests show flakiness in production CI.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/008-spec-memory-mcp-stress-test/evidence/checkpoint-create-rca.md:5:1. **Likely: SQLite write-lock / busy failure during a large checkpoint transaction under paired-parallel sweep load. Confidence: 65%.**  
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/008-spec-memory-mcp-stress-test/evidence/checkpoint-create-rca.md:55:4. For sweep fixtures, prefer scoped checkpoints or `includeEmbeddings=false` where rollback semantics allow it. A global embedding-inclusive checkpoint is expensive enough that parallel sweep harnesses should treat it as a serialized lifecycle operation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/008-spec-memory-mcp-stress-test/evidence/checkpoint-create-rca.md:59:**P1.** This does not corrupt existing data and the pre-sweep rollback checkpoint still exists, so it is not P0. It does block lifecycle rollback tooling under exactly the conditions where checkpoint reliability matters most: destructive or parallel maintenance sweeps. The hidden underlying DB error also slows recovery because operators see only `CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED`.

Live probes captured during the 006 sweep show the runtime still serves pre-fix behavior:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/020-enterprise-readiness-verification-expansion-research/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:38:Iteration 10 reached. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:88:**Council convergence**: Lenses A (safety), B (defensive), C (UX), D (migration) all endorsed full snapshot. Lens E (devil's advocate) initially objected ("snapshots are theater") but conceded that WAL handles single-DB crash, not cross-DB inconsistency.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:102:**Rationale**: A fully exclusive lock is impractical for SQLite WAL (connections are file-handle-level, not lock-level for readers). Strict refusal (Lens A's initial position) is too restrictive given the user's parallel-session workflow. Warn-and-prompt with `--force` override balances safety with operator workflow.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:104:**Council convergence**: Lens A revised from "refuse" to "warn-and-prompt" after Lens C challenged usability. Lens B's `flock(2)` proposal accepted as primary primitive (cross-platform via `portalocker`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:120:**Council convergence**: Lens E (devil's advocate) argued "just retry the failed step" — accepted partially as one retry, but with rollback as the floor after retry fails.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:134:**Rationale**: `flock(2)` is OS-enforced and immune to PID-reuse races. PID-file alone is vulnerable to recycled PIDs after a crash. Cross-platform `portalocker` provides the Windows path. The user's parallel-session workflow (per memory `feedback_worktree_cleanliness_not_a_blocker`) makes this a real risk, not a theoretical one.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:136:**Council convergence**: All five lenses agreed. Lens E (devil's advocate) had no objection — concurrent invocations are user error worth refusing loudly.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:158:**Council convergence**: Lens C (UX advocate) introduced tiering after Lens A/B initial split between strict-confirm vs match-existing-pattern. Lens E (devil.s advocate) wanted unattended default — rejected because this command is rare-use, not routine.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:174:**Council convergence**: Lens A, B, C, D all endorsed graceful cancel. Lens E objected ("just don't allow cancellation") but conceded that 15-min lock-up with no escape is unacceptable UX.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:192:**Council convergence**: Lens E (devil's advocate) had the strongest position here ("docs page, no deletion engine") — partially accepted as detect-and-recommend rather than detect-and-delete.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:206:**Default if not dispatched**: Document waiver here with rationale (e.g., "internal council convergence was strong; external dispatch deferred to follow-on packet if Q3 ADR-001 finding contradicts the council's per-batch assumption").
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/001-implement-initial-doctor-command-set/decision-record.md:212:**Council convergence quality**: Genuine. Lens E (devil's advocate) endorsed most of A/B/C/D's positions after challenges, not artificially. Operational-safety-of-database-orchestration is a domain where conservative defaults are objectively correct (asymmetric cost: 5 min annoyance vs hours of data loss).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/001-search-intelligence-stress-playbook/002-search-scenario-execution/plan.md:126:### Phase 3: Verification (deferred to execution session)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/spec.md:64:The v1.0.1 stress-test sweep (`../001-search-intelligence-stress-playbook/002-search-scenario-execution/findings.md`) baselined the four MCP-runtime surfaces against a 30-cell scenario matrix on 2026-04-26 and surfaced the regressions that drove the eight remediation packets shipped earlier this session (003-009 in the renumbered phase tree, originally tracked as 008-014). All eight remediation packets landed source patches plus rebuilt `dist/`, and live MCP probes earlier this session confirmed the fork dist is loaded in production. What is *not yet measured* is the impact: did the contract additions (`preEnforcementTokens`, `fallbackDecision`, `path_class` rerank, `responsePolicy.noCanonicalPathClaims`, normalized `IntentTelemetry`, `deltaByRelation`, daemon rebuild protocol) actually move the per-cell quality, latency, and hallucination scores compared to the v1.0.1 baseline? Without that measurement, the remediation cycle is "shipped but unconfirmed" and HANDOVER-deferred.md §2.1 stays open indefinitely.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/spec.md:67:Re-run the same 30-cell sweep on the post-fix dist, score it under the same v1.0.1 rubric (4 dimensions, 0-2 scale, 8 pts max per cell — no recalibration; the rubric stays canonical so deltas are directly comparable), and aggregate per-cell deltas into per-packet verdicts. Headline question this packet exists to answer: **"For each remediation packet 003-009, did at least one cell improve by ≥1 point under the v1.0.1 rubric?"** A YES across the board closes HANDOVER-deferred §2.1 with green evidence; any NO is an indicator that the source-code patches shipped but didn't reach the runtime path the rubric exercises.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/spec.md:109:| `../HANDOVER-deferred.md` | Modify | §2.1 status: `Pending` → `Scaffolded — see ./010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/`. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/spec.md:148:| REQ-014 | I2 prompt forces weak-quality path | The I2 prompt OR a deterministic preamble guarantees `memory_search` returns `quality:weak` so REQ-011 can be exercised. v1.0.1 I2 happened to land in weak path organically; v1.0.2 must guarantee it (this is the sub-target from `../HANDOVER-deferred.md` §2.1 baseline). |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/spec.md:171:| 006 | causal-graph-window-metrics | Indirect: any cell relying on causal-graph signal cleanliness — primarily Q2 (vague search system) where causal edges support graph traversal | Q2: 5/5/6/n-a | NEUTRAL expected; this packet is detection-only at default cap — actual quality lift requires production tuning (HANDOVER-deferred §2.2) |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/010-stress-test-close-loop-measurement-rerun/spec.md:234:- If v1.0.2 surfaces new defects not covered by 003-009, do they belong in HANDOVER-deferred.md or a new packet under 011? .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:34:approaching-convergence
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/review/INCIDENT-2026-05-11-rereview-partial-loss.md:32:Concurrent parallel-session activity on `main`. Between worktree creation (commit `37ea931ce` at ~11:11 UTC local) and the loop driver's completion (~12:28 UTC local), 5 unrelated parallel commits landed on main (`b98945e62`, `4bf78a516`, `9ca34b1ec`, `2908deb22`, `a02e7d009`). The parallel session also appears to have stomped the worktree directory `/013-doctor-rereview/` — its `.git` pointer was removed and most of its files were deleted while the deep-review loop was still running. Files written AFTER the destruction wave (iter-10 outputs) survived because they were created in the residual directory after the cleanup occurred.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/review/INCIDENT-2026-05-11-rereview-partial-loss.md:42:3. The **iter-10 narrative** survived and contains the full Closure Verification table across all 10 clusters (A-J), all 4 P2 findings with full CLAIM ADJUDICATION schema, regression check, traceability checks, deferred findings list, and final verdict.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/002-doctor-update-orchestrator/review/INCIDENT-2026-05-11-rereview-partial-loss.md:60:2. **Worktree isolation is not bulletproof against concurrent session interference.** A parallel session that touches git worktree management can stomp another session's worktree. For high-stakes multi-iteration dispatches, schedule them when no concurrent worktree-modifying activity is expected, OR add a worktree lock primitive (out of scope for this packet).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/015-cocoindex-seed-telemetry-passthrough/implementation-summary.md:91:Implemented directly against `spec.md`, `tasks.md`, and research.md §6 / Q-OPP. Strictly observed scope discipline: no `mcp_server/lib/search/*` change (research.md §6.3 alt #3 REJECTED), no fork change (frozen at `0.2.3+spec-kit-fork.0.2.0`), no response-level `dedupedAliases`/`uniqueResultCount` change (research.md §6.3 alt #2 deferred), no Stage 3 reranking change.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/015-cocoindex-seed-telemetry-passthrough/implementation-summary.md:142:2. **Response-level telemetry deferred.** `dedupedAliases` and `uniqueResultCount` are response-level counters (research.md §6.3 alt #2). They cannot be reconstructed from individual seeds and would require the caller to supply the entire CocoIndex response envelope into `code_graph_context` — a separate composition decision and out of scope for this packet.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/015-cocoindex-seed-telemetry-passthrough/implementation-summary.md:143:3. **textBrief / combinedSummary unchanged.** Telemetry surfaces only on the `anchors[]` array of the response, not in the human-readable `textBrief` or `combinedSummary`. Surfacing telemetry in narrative output is an open question (spec §7) deferred to a follow-on packet.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/011-research-post-stress-finding-followups/research/research.md:59:| Iterations | 10/10 (max-iterations hit, NOT premature convergence) |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/011-research-post-stress-finding-followups/research/research.md:65:**newInfoRatio trajectory**: 0.74 → 0.58 → 0.46 → 0.41 → 0.46 → 0.38 → 0.34 → 0.46 → 0.27 → 0.22. The evidence shows an overall downward trajectory with two rebounds, not strict monotonic decay. The brief +0.05 lift at iter-5 reflects the `Q-OPP` reframing from "duplicated rerank?" → "seed-fidelity gap"; the iter-8 rebound similarly surfaced late explanatory detail rather than a contradiction. The endpoint still supports convergence: no contradictions after iteration 7 and the final two iterations drop to 0.27 → 0.22.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/research/002-copilot-hook-followup-deep-review-remediation/deep-research-strategy.md:56:- `001-copilot-hook-gap-deep-review-remediation` converged on Outcome B (file-based workaround) in April 2026 with 30+ primary-source citations. It did NOT investigate the hook-config schema — only confirmed that Copilot customer hooks cannot mutate prompts per GitHub docs.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/002-mcp-runtime-improvement-research/research/001-mcp-runtime-improvement-research-pass/iterations/iteration-008.md:9:1. Re-read the 005 evidence for REQ-010 and the deferred Cluster 7 task notes.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/002-mcp-runtime-improvement-research/research/001-mcp-runtime-improvement-research-pass/iterations/iteration-008.md:27:The save path has a direct automatic `supersedes` producer. `resolveCreateRecordLineage()` turns prediction-error `SUPERSEDE` decisions into a `causalSupersedesMemoryId` when the selected existing memory differs from the same-path predecessor (`mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts:129-140`). `recordCrossPathSupersedesEdge()` then inserts a `SUPERSEDES` edge with `createdBy: "auto"` (`mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts:174-196`), and the created record calls it after lineage transition (`mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts:386-396`). The older memory-save path has the same behavior in `recordCrossPathPeSupersedes()` (`mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts:589-612`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/002-mcp-runtime-improvement-research/research/001-mcp-runtime-improvement-research-pass/iterations/iteration-008.md:29:Reconsolidation also creates `supersedes` edges as a first-class lifecycle action. Merge creates `new -> existing` supersedes before recording lineage (`mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts:355-365`), conflict handling does the same for distinct new IDs (`mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts:491-539`), and the helper uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` for relation `supersedes` (`mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts:1120-1152`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/002-mcp-runtime-improvement-research/research/001-mcp-runtime-improvement-research-pass/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:`insertEdge()` caps automatic edge strength at 0.5 and rejects auto edges after a source node reaches `MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE` (`mcp_server/lib/storage/causal-edges.ts:234-253`). That guards per-node fanout. It does not cap a relation class across a save batch, time window, scan cycle, or corpus. Hundreds of distinct new/source nodes can still add hundreds of `supersedes` edges.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/changelog/changelog-008-docs-impact-remediation.md:23:Sub-packets 001 through 011 changed runtime hook contracts, advisor delivery, plugin-loader semantics, Copilot wrapper schema, Codex startup parity, and Claude prompt-time hook registration. The shipped behavior diverged from what the checked-in documentation still described. Ten parallel `cli-codex` impact-analysis agents audited every referenced path and flagged 13 files for update (10 HIGH, 3 MED). This phase updated all 13 files to match the shipped behavior, with a post-05 Codex reconciliation rule embedded in every agent prompt to ensure no doc still said "Codex has no lifecycle hook."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/001-fix-runtime-hook-parity-findings/spec.md:208:| R-002 | Strict validation fixes accidentally overstate completion. | Medium | Medium | Mark only evidence-backed tasks `[x]`; keep true blockers documented as deferred. |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/research/002-copilot-hook-followup-deep-review-remediation/iterations/iteration-006.md:153:Research-only convergence is met. The loop now has:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/changelog/changelog-002-copilot-hook-parity-remediation-research-pt-03.md:23:An 8-iteration deep research session converged on the root cause of the `Neither 'bash' nor 'powershell' specified in hook command configuration` error that Copilot CLI 1.0.34 threw on every user prompt. The crash was traced to Copilot merging hook configs from both `.github/hooks/*.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json`. The Claude-style matcher wrappers had no top-level `bash` or `powershell` field, and Copilot's executor `g2()` threw when processing those wrappers. The `sessionStart` event escaped because it filters by `type === "command"` before `g2()`, but `userPromptSubmitted` does not. The error had existed since Copilot 1.0.14 and was not a 1.0.34 regression.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/changelog/changelog-002-copilot-hook-parity-remediation-research-pt-03.md:39:- Research report at `002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/research/002-copilot-hook-followup-deep-review-remediation/research.md` (8 iterations, converged at iter-8).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/003-install-scripts-doctor-realignment/004-deferred-followups-and-cleanup/implementation-summary.md:65:Added `## 1. OVERVIEW` (Purpose / When to Use / Key …) to `state_jsonl.md`, `loop_state_and_gates.md`, `convergence_recovery.md`, renumbering existing sections, matching the sibling shape in `convergence.md`/`loop_protocol.md`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/deep-research-strategy.md:10:What is the exact Codex CLI (codex-cli 0.122.0) hook contract that `~/.codex/hooks.json` registers commands against? Specifically: (a) what stdin payload format do hook commands receive; (b) is stdout injected into the model context or is it purely terminal-notification; (c) what do exit codes mean (block vs log-only); (d) what's the timeout budget; (e) when multiple hooks are registered for one event, is execution serial/parallel and in what order; (f) what's the full event taxonomy beyond SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop; (g) can a UserPromptSubmit hook decline/transform the prompt before it reaches the model (Claude's "blocker" pattern); (h) do hooks run with full env vars?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/deep-research-strategy.md:31:- Implementation (this is the research/contract phase; port is Phase 2 work after this converges).

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/research.md:99:`PermissionRequest` has its own parallel decision contract (JSON `{"decision":"deny", "reason":"..."}` or exit 2; any deny wins over allow). `PreToolUse` has an `updatedInput` field but it's parsed-but-unsupported and fails open.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/research.md:129:Source: `execute_handlers` uses `futures::future::join_all(...)` to launch all matching handlers at once. Result aggregation preserves selected handler order for folding, but execution is parallel. Implication: when the user's existing Superset `notify.sh` and a new Spec Kit Memory hook are both registered for the same event, they run in parallel. Neither can prevent the other from starting. For this spec: Spec Kit Memory's hook must be idempotent and must not depend on notify.sh having already completed.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-operator-tooling/001-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/research.md:189:| Q5 | Multi-hook ordering? | Concurrent via `join_all`, NOT serial. Aggregation preserves handler order for folding but execution is parallel. | F6 |

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	name: deep-loop-runtime
     3	version: 1.4.0
     4	description: "Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing."
     5	allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]
     6	---
     7	
     8	# Deep Loop Runtime
     9	
    10	Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.
    11	
    12	---
    13	
    14	## 1. WHEN TO USE
    15	
    16	This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.
    17	
    18	Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:
    19	
    20	- executor configuration and audit metadata
    21	- prompt-pack rendering
    22	- post-dispatch validation
    23	- atomic state files and JSONL repair
    24	- loop locking and permissions gates
    25	- Bayesian scoring and fallback routing
    26	- coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation
    27	
    28	### Activation Triggers
    29	
    30	This skill is activated when:
    31	
    32	- A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`
    33	- A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`
    34	- An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts
    35	- A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing
    36	
    37	### When NOT to Use
    38	
    39	Do not invoke this skill when:
    40	
    41	- A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)
    42	- A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)
    43	- An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)
    44	
    45	---
    46	
    47	## 2. SMART ROUTING
    48	
    49	This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.
    50	
    51	### Primary Detection Signal
    52	
    53	```bash
    54	request_text="$(printf '%s' "$USER_REQUEST" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
    55	case "$request_text" in
    56	  *"deep-loop runtime"*|*"deep-loop-runtime"*|*"executor config"*|*"prompt pack"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
    57	  *"coverage graph"*|*"deep_loop_graph"*|*"bayesian scorer"*|*"loop lock"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
    58	  *"fallback router"*|*"post-dispatch validate"*|*"jsonl repair"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
    59	  *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;
    60	esac
    61	```
    62	
    63	### Phase Detection
    64	
    65	```text
    66	TASK CONTEXT
    67	    |
    68	    +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent
    69	    +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs
    70	    +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree
    71	    +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair
    72	```
    73	
    74	### Resource Domains
    75	
    76	```text
    77	lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)
    78	lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules
    79	scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)
    80	database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)
    81	tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility
    82	```
    83	
    84	### Resource Loading Levels
    85	
    86	| Tier | When loaded | Purpose |
    87	|------|-------------|---------|
    88	| ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |
    89	| CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |
    90	| ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |
    91	
    92	### Smart Router Pseudocode
    93	
    94	```text
    95	IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. "bayesian-scorer", "prompt-pack"):
    96	  → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts
    97	ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:
    98	  → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts
    99	ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):
   100	  → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts
   101	ELSE:
   102	  → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)
   103	```
   104	
   105	---
   106	
   107	## 3. HOW IT WORKS
   108	
   109	### Runtime Architecture
   110	
   111	The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).
   112	
   113	```text
   114	[deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]
   115	            │
   116	            ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)
   117	            │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite
   118	            │
   119	            └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)
   120	                    └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.
   121	```
   122	
   123	### Library Structure
   124	
   125	**`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**
   126	- `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings
   127	- `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)
   128	- `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template
   129	- `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)
   130	- `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes
   131	- `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines
   132	- `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking
   133	- `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks
   134	- `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring
   135	- `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix
   136	
   137	**`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**
   138	- `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle
   139	- `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders
   140	- `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction
   141	
   142	### Council Primitives
   143	
   144	The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.
   145	
   146	**`lib/council/` (5 modules):**
   147	- `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.
   148	- `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.
   149	- `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.
   150	- `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.
   151	- `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.
   152	
   153	The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.
   154	
   155	### Script Entry Points
   156	
   157	Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):
   158	
   159	| Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |
   160	|--------|----------|---------|------------|
   161	| `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
   162	| `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
   163	| `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
   164	| `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |
   165	| `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |
   166	| `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |
   167	| `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |
   168	| `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |
   169	
   170	**Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.
   171	
   172	Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.
   173	
   174	### Test Organization
   175	
   176	```text
   177	tests/
   178	├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)
   179	├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures
   180	├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification
   181	└── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests
   182	```
   183	
   184	The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.
   185	
   186	---
   187	
   188	## 4. RULES
   189	
   190	### ALWAYS
   191	
   192	- **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)
   193	- **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)
   194	- **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)
   195	- **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)
   196	- **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write
   197	- **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation
   198	
   199	### NEVER
   200	
   201	- **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction
   202	- **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection
   203	- **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead
   204	- **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`
   205	- **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix
   206	
   207	### ESCALATE IF
   208	
   209	- **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override
   210	- **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR
   211	- **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`
   212	- **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape
   213	
   214	---
   215	
   216	## 5. SCOPE
   217	
   218	In scope:
   219	
   220	- runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`
