     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
     2	
     3	import { z } from '../../../system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules/zod/index.js';
     4	
     5	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     6	
     7	export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-gemini', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode', 'cli-devin'] as const;
     8	export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
     9	
    10	export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
    11	export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
    12	
    13	export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
    14	export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
    15	
    16	const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
    17	export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
    18	export type GeminiSandboxMode = 'docker' | 'none';
    19	export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
    20	
    21	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    22	
    23	export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
    24	  kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
    25	  model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    26	  reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
    27	  serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
    28	  sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
    29	  timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
    30	});
    31	
    32	export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
    33	
    34	export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
    35	  native: [],
    36	  'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    37	  'cli-gemini': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    38	  'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    39	  'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    40	  'cli-devin': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    41	};
    42	
    43	export const GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['gemini-3.1-pro-preview'] as const;
    44	export type GeminiSupportedModel = typeof GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    45	
    46	export const DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['swe-1.6', 'deepseek-v4', 'glm-5.1', 'kimi-k2.6'] as const;
    47	export type DevinSupportedModel = typeof DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    48	export type DevinPermissionMode = 'auto' | 'dangerous';
    49	
    50	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    51	
    52	type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
    53	  path: PropertyKey[];
    54	  message: string;
    55	};
    56	
    57	export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
    58	  kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>;
    59	
    60	  constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>) {
    61	    super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
    62	    this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
    63	    this.kind = kind;
    64	  }
    65	}
    66	
    67	export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
    68	  issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
    69	
    70	  constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
    71	    super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
    72	    this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
    73	    this.issues = input.issues;
    74	  }
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    78	
    79	function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    80	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
    81	}
    82	
    83	function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
    84	  return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
    85	}
    86	
    87	function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
    88	  if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
    89	    return raw;
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  const legacyKind = raw.type;
    93	  if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
    94	    return raw;
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
    98	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
    99	      issues: [{
   100	        path: ['type'],
   101	        message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
   102	      }],
   103	    });
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
   107	  const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
   108	  return {
   109	    ...rest,
   110	    kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
   111	  };
   112	}
   113	
   114	function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
   115	  return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
   116	    path: issue.path,
   117	    message: issue.message,
   118	  }));
   119	}
   120	
   121	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
   125	 *
   126	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   127	 * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
   128	 */
   129	export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
   130	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/**
   134	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Gemini CLI sandbox mode.
   135	 *
   136	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   137	 * @returns 'docker' for workspace-write/read-only, 'none' for danger-full-access.
   138	 */
   139	export function resolveGeminiSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): GeminiSandboxMode {
   140	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'none' : 'docker';
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
   145	 *
   146	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   147	 * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
   148	 */
   149	export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
   150	  switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
   151	    case 'read-only':
   152	      return 'plan';
   153	    case 'danger-full-access':
   154	      return 'bypassPermissions';
   155	    default:
   156	      return 'acceptEdits';
   157	  }
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Devin CLI permission mode.
   162	 *
   163	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   164	 * @returns 'auto' for workspace-write/read-only, 'dangerous' for danger-full-access.
   165	 */
   166	export function resolveDevinPermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): DevinPermissionMode {
   167	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'dangerous' : 'auto';
   168	}
   169	
   170	/**
   171	 * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
   172	 *
   173	 * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
   174	 * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
   175	 *
   176	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   177	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   178	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   179	 */
   180	export function parseExecutorConfig(raw: unknown): ExecutorConfig {
   181	  const parsed = executorConfigSchema.safeParse(normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw));
   182	  if (!parsed.success) {
   183	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   184	      issues: normalizeIssues(parsed.error),
   185	    });
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  const config = parsed.data;
   189	  if (config.kind === 'cli-codex' && config.model === null) {
   190	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   191	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-codex' }],
   192	    });
   193	  }
   194	  if (config.kind === 'cli-devin' && config.model === null) {
   195	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   196	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-devin' }],
   197	    });
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  const supportedFlags = EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT[config.kind];
   201	  const unsupportedFields: string[] = [];
   202	  const allOptionalFields: (keyof ExecutorConfig)[] = [
   203	    'model',
   204	    'reasoningEffort',
   205	    'serviceTier',
   206	    'sandboxMode',
   207	    'timeoutSeconds',
   208	  ];
   209	
   210	  for (const field of allOptionalFields) {
   211	    if (!supportedFlags.includes(field) && config[field] !== null && !(field === 'timeoutSeconds' && config[field] === 900)) {
   212	      unsupportedFields.push(field);
   213	    }
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  if (unsupportedFields.length > 0) {
   217	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   218	      issues: unsupportedFields.map((field) => ({
   219	        path: [field],
   220	        message: `field '${field}' is not supported by executor kind '${config.kind}'. Supported fields for ${config.kind}: ${
   221	          supportedFlags.length ? supportedFlags.join(', ') : 'none'
   222	        }.`,
   223	      })),
   224	    });
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  if (
   228	    config.kind === 'cli-gemini' &&
   229	    config.model !== null &&
   230	    !GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   231	  ) {
   232	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   233	      issues: [
   234	        {
   235	          path: ['model'],
   236	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-gemini model. Supported: ${GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   237	        },
   238	      ],
   239	    });
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  if (
   243	    config.kind === 'cli-devin' &&
   244	    config.model !== null &&
   245	    !DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   246	  ) {
   247	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   248	      issues: [
   249	        {
   250	          path: ['model'],
   251	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-devin model. Supported: ${DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   252	        },
   253	      ],
   254	    });
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  return config;
   258	}
   259	
   260	/**

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Cross-Lineage Merge                          ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Input:  CLI args (--loop-type, --artifact-dir).                          ║
     5	// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
     6	// ║ Exit:   0=ok, 1=script error, 3=input validation error.                 ║
     7	// ║                                                                          ║
     8	// ║ Reads every {artifact-dir}/lineages/{label}/ sub-packet and produces:   ║
     9	// ║   research: deduplicated deep-research-findings-registry.json +          ║
    10	// ║             fanout-attribution.md                                        ║
    11	// ║   review:   severity-rollup deep-review-findings-registry.json           ║
    12	// ║             (strongest-restriction: any lineage P0 → merged FAIL) +     ║
    13	// ║             fanout-attribution.md                                        ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	const fs = require('node:fs');
    19	const path = require('node:path');
    20	
    21	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22	// 1. HELPERS
    23	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24	
    25	function inputError(message) {
    26	  const err = new Error(message);
    27	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    28	  return err;
    29	}
    30	
    31	function jsonOut(payload) {
    32	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
    33	}
    34	
    35	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    36	  const args = {};
    37	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    38	    const token = argv[i];
    39	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    40	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    41	    }
    42	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    43	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    44	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    45	      args[key] = true;
    46	    } else {
    47	      args[key] = next;
    48	      i += 1;
    49	    }
    50	  }
    51	  return args;
    52	}
    53	
    54	function ensureString(args, key) {
    55	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    56	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
    57	  }
    58	  return args[key];
    59	}
    60	
    61	function tryReadJson(filePath) {
    62	  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return null;
    63	  try {
    64	    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
    65	  } catch {
    66	    return null;
    67	  }
    68	}
    69	
    70	function readStateLog(stateLogPath) {
    71	  if (!fs.existsSync(stateLogPath)) return [];
    72	  const lines = fs.readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean);
    73	  return lines.flatMap((line) => {
    74	    try {
    75	      return [JSON.parse(line)];
    76	    } catch {
    77	      return [];
    78	    }
    79	  });
    80	}
    81	
    82	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	// 2. RESEARCH MERGE
    84	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85	
    86	/**
    87	 * Merge research findings registries from all lineages.
    88	 * Deduplicates by findingId; cross-model attribution via lineage labels.
    89	 * Returns the merged registry object.
    90	 */
    91	function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData) {
    92	  const findingById = new Map();
    93	
    94	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
    95	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.keyFindings)) continue;
    96	    for (const finding of registry.keyFindings) {
    97	      const id = finding.id || finding.title;
    98	      if (!id) continue;
    99	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   100	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   101	        // Add this lineage as a contributing source
   102	        if (!existing._lineages) existing._lineages = [];
   103	        if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   104	      } else {
   105	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   106	      }
   107	    }
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   111	  const openQuestionsById = new Map();
   112	  const ruledOutById = new Map();
   113	
   114	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   115	    if (!registry) continue;
   116	    for (const q of registry.openQuestions ?? []) {
   117	      const id = q.id || q.question;
   118	      if (!id) continue;
   119	      if (!openQuestionsById.has(id)) openQuestionsById.set(id, { ...q, _lineages: [label] });
   120	      else openQuestionsById.get(id)._lineages.push(label);
   121	    }
   122	    for (const d of registry.ruledOutDirections ?? []) {
   123	      const id = d.id || d.direction;
   124	      if (!id) continue;
   125	      if (!ruledOutById.has(id)) ruledOutById.set(id, { ...d, _lineages: [label] });
   126	    }
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  const totalIters = lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => {
   130	    return sum + (registry?.metrics?.iterationsCompleted ?? 0);
   131	  }, 0);
   132	
   133	  const avgConvergence =
   134	    lineageData.length > 0
   135	      ? lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => sum + (registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? 0), 0) /
   136	        lineageData.length
   137	      : 0;
   138	
   139	  return {
   140	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   141	    openQuestions: [...openQuestionsById.values()],
   142	    resolvedQuestions: [],
   143	    keyFindings: mergedFindings,
   144	    ruledOutDirections: [...ruledOutById.values()],
   145	    metrics: {
   146	      iterationsCompleted: totalIters,
   147	      openQuestions: openQuestionsById.size,
   148	      resolvedQuestions: 0,
   149	      keyFindings: mergedFindings.length,
   150	      convergenceScore: Math.round(avgConvergence * 1000) / 1000,
   151	      coverageBySources: {},
   152	    },
   153	  };
   154	}
   155	
   156	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   157	// 3. REVIEW MERGE  (strongest-restriction)
   158	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   159	
   160	const SEVERITY_RANK = { P0: 3, P1: 2, P2: 1 };
   161	
   162	/**
   163	 * Merge review findings registries with strongest-restriction severity rollup.
   164	 * Any lineage with an active P0 finding causes the merged result to be FAIL.
   165	 * Deduplication is by findingId; cross-lineage P0 wins if any lineage reports it.
   166	 */
   167	function mergeReviewRegistries(lineageData) {
   168	  const findingById = new Map();
   169	
   170	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   171	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.openFindings)) continue;
   172	    for (const finding of registry.openFindings) {
   173	      if (finding.status !== 'active') continue;
   174	      const id = finding.findingId || finding.title;
   175	      if (!id) continue;
   176	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   177	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   178	        // Strongest-restriction: escalate to highest severity seen across lineages
   179	        if ((SEVERITY_RANK[finding.severity] ?? 0) > (SEVERITY_RANK[existing.severity] ?? 0)) {
   180	          findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [...(existing._lineages || []), label] });
   181	        } else {
   182	          if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   183	        }
   184	      } else {
   185	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   186	      }
   187	    }
   188	  }
   189	
   190	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   191	  const activeP0 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P0' && f.status === 'active').length;
   192	  const activeP1 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P1' && f.status === 'active').length;
   193	  const activeP2 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P2' && f.status === 'active').length;
   194	
   195	  // Strongest-restriction verdict
   196	  let mergedVerdict;
   197	  if (activeP0 > 0) {
   198	    mergedVerdict = 'FAIL';
   199	  } else if (activeP1 > 0) {
   200	    mergedVerdict = 'CONDITIONAL';
   201	  } else {
   202	    mergedVerdict = 'PASS';
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  return {
   206	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   207	    mergedVerdict,
   208	    openFindings: mergedFindings,
   209	    resolvedFindings: [],
   210	    findingsBySeverity: { P0: activeP0, P1: activeP1, P2: activeP2 },
   211	    openFindingsCount: mergedFindings.length,
   212	    resolvedFindingsCount: 0,
   213	    activeP0,
   214	    activeP1,
   215	    activeP2,
   216	  };
   217	}
   218	
   219	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   220	// 4. ATTRIBUTION
   221	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Build fanout-attribution.md summarizing per-lineage convergence, iters, salvage, model.
   225	 */
   226	function buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType) {
   227	  const lines = [
   228	    `# Fan-Out Attribution`,
   229	    ``,
   230	    `Loop type: **${loopType}**  |  Lineage count: **${lineageData.length}**`,
   231	    ``,
   232	    `| Label | Kind | Model | Iterations | Convergence | Salvaged | Verdict |`,
   233	    `|-------|------|-------|-----------|-------------|----------|---------|`,
   234	  ];
   235	
   236	  for (const { label, registry, stateRecords, kind, model } of lineageData) {
   237	    const iters = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'iteration').length;
   238	    const salvage = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'salvaged_from_stdout').length;
   239	    const convergenceScore = registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? registry?.convergenceScore ?? 'n/a';
   240	    const verdict =
   241	      loopType === 'review'
   242	        ? registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P0 > 0
   243	          ? 'FAIL'
   244	          : registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P1 > 0
   245	          ? 'CONDITIONAL'
   246	          : 'PASS'
   247	        : 'n/a';
   248	    lines.push(`| ${label} | ${kind ?? 'unknown'} | ${model ?? 'default'} | ${iters} | ${convergenceScore} | ${salvage} | ${verdict} |`);
   249	  }
   250	
   251	  lines.push('');
   252	  return lines.join('\n');
   253	}
   254	
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	// 5. MAIN
   257	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   258	
   259	function main() {
   260	  const args = parseArgs();
   261	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   262	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   263	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   264	  }
   265	  const artifactDir = ensureString(args, 'artifactDir');
   266	  const lineagesDir = path.join(artifactDir, 'lineages');
   267	
   268	  if (!fs.existsSync(lineagesDir)) {
   269	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineages directory — nothing to merge', merged: 0 });
   270	    return;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  const labelDirs = fs.readdirSync(lineagesDir).filter((entry) =>
   274	    fs.statSync(path.join(lineagesDir, entry)).isDirectory(),
   275	  );
   276	
   277	  if (labelDirs.length === 0) {
   278	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineage subdirs found', merged: 0 });
   279	    return;
   280	  }

 succeeded in 0ms:
   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   261	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   262	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   263	  }
   264	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
   265	  const baseArtifactDir = ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir');
   266	
   267	  const {
   268	    parseFanoutConfig,
   269	    expandLineages,
   270	    resolveCodexSandboxMode,
   271	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
   272	    resolveGeminiSandboxMode,
   273	    resolveDevinPermissionMode,
   274	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
   275	
   276	  installSignalHandlers(() => {});
   277	  maybeThrowTestFault();
   278	
   279	  let rawConfig;
   280	  try {
   281	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
   282	  } catch {
   283	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
   284	  }
   285	
   286	  const fanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
   287	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
   288	
   289	  // Fan-out pool handles CLI lineages only; native lineages are dispatched by the YAML step.
   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };
   361	    },
   362	  });
   363	
   364	  writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
   365	    run_id: runId,
   366	    loop_type: loopType,
   367	    spec_folder: specFolder,
   368	    base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
   369	    total_cli_lineages: cliLineages.length,
   370	    ...summary,
   371	  });
   372	
   373	  const exitCode = summary.all_failed ? 3 : summary.failed > 0 ? 2 : 0;
   374	  jsonOut({ status: exitCode === 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial', run_id: runId, results, summary });
   375	  process.exit(exitCode);
   376	}
   377	
   378	main().catch((err) => {
   379	  const code = classifyExitCode(err);
   380	  jsonOut({
   381	    status: 'error',
   382	    error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
   383	    code: err && err.code ? err.code : 'SCRIPT_ERROR',
   384	  });
   385	  if (code === 1) {
   386	    process.stderr.write(
   387	      JSON.stringify({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), stack: err && err.stack }) + '\n',
   388	    );
   389	  }
   390	  process.exit(code);
   391	});

    49	
    50	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    51

    59	
    60	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-research-config.json` (shape: `researchTopic`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.

Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: none needed. Missing `research_topic` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.

    64	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-research-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    65	
    66	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the consolidated setup prompt section below.
    67	
    68	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    69	
    70	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.
    71	
    72	```yaml
    73	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    74	  research_topic: WebSocket reconnection strategies  # string
    75	  spec_folder: <spec-folder>  # existing | new | update-related | phase-folder | explicit path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix

    86	
    87	Rules: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2 (unspecified fields fall back to default; marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags; unknown fields warn; malformed lines parse-error).
    88	
    89	### Default Resolution Table
    90	
    91	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    92	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
    93	| `research_topic` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` positional topic, or marker `research_topic` | none | N |
    94	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or targeted choice among suggested existing/new/update-related/phase folder | none | Y, when topic is present but folder choice is ambiguous |

    96	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `10` | N |
    97	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.05` | N |
    98	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
    99	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |

   106

Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.

   112	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   113	
   114	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   115	   |-- ":auto"         -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   116	   |-- ":confirm"      -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   117	   +-- No suffix       -> execution_mode = "ASK"
   118	
   119	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for topic:
   120	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   121	   |     -> research_topic = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   122	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   123	
   124	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   125	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   126	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   127	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   128	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   129	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   130	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   131	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   132	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   133	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   134	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   135	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   136	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
   137	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   138	   |
   139	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   140	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   141	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   142	   |
   143	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   144	
   145	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   146	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   147	   - The generated `deep-research-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   148	
   149	   Parsing to config mapping:
   150	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   151	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   152	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   153	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   154	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
   155	
   156	   Validation hook:
   157	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
   158	   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
   159	
   160	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
   161	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
   162	
   163	5. Search for prior work (background):
   164	   - memory_context({ input: research_topic OR "deep-research", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
   165	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
   166	
   167	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
   168	   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the topic text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
   169	   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`

    12	> Under `:auto`, setup follows the three-tier resolution contract in §0: resolve confidently, ask only targeted ambiguity questions, then fail fast if required inputs remain unresolved. Under `:confirm`, setup keeps the consolidated interactive question block.
    13	>
    14	> **YOUR FIRST ACTION:**
    15	> 1. Run the unified setup phase in this Markdown entrypoint and resolve:
    16	>    - `review_target`
    17	>    - `review_target_type`
    18	>    - `review_dimensions`
    19	>    - `spec_folder`
    20	>    - `execution_mode`
    21	>    - `maxIterations`
    22	>    - `convergenceThreshold`
    23	> 2. Load the corresponding YAML file from `assets/` only after all setup values are resolved:
    24	>    - Auto: `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`
    25	>    - Confirm: `deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml`
    26	> 3. Execute the YAML workflow step by step using those resolved values
    27	>
    28	> All content below is reference context for the YAML workflow. Do not treat reference sections as direct instructions to execute.
    29	
    30	## CONSTRAINTS
    31	
    32	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    33	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    34	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    35	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    36	  - `review_target`, `review_target_type`, `review_dimensions`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    37	
    38	> **Canonical mode syntax:** use attached command suffixes (`/deep:start-review-loop:auto`, `/deep:start-review-loop:confirm`) and keep AGENTS, skills, and quick references synchronized to this entrypoint.
    39	
    40	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    41	
    42	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    47	
    48	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    49	
    50	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
    51	
    52	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
    53	
    54	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
    55	
    56	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
    57	
    58	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
    59	
    60	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `review_target_type`, `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: if `review_target_type` is ambiguous, ask only for `review_target_type` first — the answer may make `spec_folder` self-evident on the next Tier 1 pass. Missing `review_target` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    61	
    62	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-review-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    63	
    64	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the Consolidated Setup Prompt section below.
    65	
    66	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    67	
    68	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.
    69	
    70	```yaml
    71	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    72	  review_target: <spec-folder>
    73	  review_target_type: spec-folder  # one of: spec-folder | skill | agent | track | files
    74	  review_dimensions: all  # or comma-separated subset: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
    75	  spec_folder: existing  # one of: existing | new | update-related | phase-folder, or an explicit specs/.opencode/specs path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.10
    79	  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # optional
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules:
    88	
    89	- Any unspecified field falls back to its documented default.
    90	- Marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags because the caller explicitly bound setup in the prompt body.
    91	- Unknown fields are warnings, not errors.
    92	- Malformed lines, including missing `:`, emit a parse error naming the offending line. Known fields parsed before the error may still be used, and unresolved fields continue to Tier 2 or Tier 3.
    93	- Empty strings count as unresolved for required fields.
    94	- Compact legacy answer strings are only for the consolidated `:confirm` prompt. They are not a `:auto` marker format.
    95	
    96	### Default Resolution Table
    97	
    98	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    99	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
   100	| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
   101	| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
   102	| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
   103	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
   104	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
   105	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
   106	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
   107	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
   108	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   109	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   110	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   111	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   112	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   113	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   114	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   115	
   116	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
   117	
   118	### Consolidated Setup Prompt for `:confirm` and No-Suffix Mode
   119	
   120	Use this block only when `execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"` or when no suffix was supplied and Q2 must ask for the execution mode.
   121	
   122	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   123	
   124	```
   125	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   126	
   127	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   128	   |-- ":auto"    -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   129	   |-- ":confirm" -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   130	   +-- No suffix  -> execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2)
   131	
   132	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for target (review_target):
   133	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   134	   |     -> review_target = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   135	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   136	
   137	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   138	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   139	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   140	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   141	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   142	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   143	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   144	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   145	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   146	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   147	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   148	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   149	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch
   150	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   151	   |
   152	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   153	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   154	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   155	   |   - Review fan-out verdict: strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL)
   156	   |
   157	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   158	
   159	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   160	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults

     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
     5	// ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
     6	// ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
     7	// ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
     8	// ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer mirroring the proven packet-122       ║
     9	// ║ orchestration-status.log.                                                  ║
    10	// ║                                                                           ║
    11	// ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
    12	// ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
    13	// ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    19	// 1. IMPORTS
    20	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	
    22	const fs = require('node:fs');
    23	const path = require('node:path');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	// 2. HELPERS
    27	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	
    29	function isRecord(value) {
    30	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    34	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    35	    return value.toISOString();
    36	  }
    37	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    38	    return value;
    39	  }
    40	  return new Date().toISOString();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    44	  const n = Number(value);
    45	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    46	    return 1;
    47	  }
    48	  return Math.floor(n);
    49	}
    50	
    51	function labelFor(item, index) {
    52	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    53	    return item.label;
    54	  }
    55	  return `item-${index}`;
    56	}
    57	
    58	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    60	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
    64	 *
    65	 * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
    66	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
    67	 *
    68	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
    69	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
    70	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
    71	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
    72	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
    73	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
    74	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
    75	 */
    76	async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
    77	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
    78	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    79	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    80	
    81	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    82	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  try {
    86	    const output = await worker(item, { index });
    87	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    88	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
    89	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    90	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
    91	    }
    92	    return {
    93	      label,
    94	      status: 'fulfilled',
    95	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    96	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
    97	      duration_ms: durationMs,
    98	      output,
    99	    };
   100	  } catch (error) {
   101	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   102	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   103	    const normalizedError = {
   104	      name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   105	      message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   106	    };
   107	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   108	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   109	    }
   110	    return {
   111	      label,
   112	      status: 'rejected',
   113	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   114	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   115	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   116	      error: normalizedError,
   117	    };
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   123	 *
   124	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   125	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   126	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   127	 *
   128	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   129	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   130	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
   131	 * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
   132	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
   133	 * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
   134	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
   135	 *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   136	 * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
   137	 */
   138	function runCappedPool(options) {
   139	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   140	    throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
   141	  }
   142	  const { items, worker } = options;
   143	  if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
   144	    throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
   145	  }
   146	  if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
   147	    throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
   151	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   152	  const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
   153	  const results = new Array(items.length);
   154	
   155	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   156	    if (items.length === 0) {
   157	      resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   158	      return;
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    let nextIndex = 0;
   162	    let active = 0;
   163	    let resolved = false;
   164	
   165	    const pump = () => {
   166	      if (resolved) {
   167	        return;
   168	      }
   169	      if (nextIndex >= items.length && active === 0) {
   170	        resolved = true;
   171	        resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   172	        return;
   173	      }
   174	      while (active < concurrency && nextIndex < items.length) {
   175	        const index = nextIndex;
   176	        nextIndex += 1;
   177	        active += 1;
   178	        settleItem({ item: items[index], index, worker, now, onEvent })
   179	          .then((result) => {
   180	            results[index] = result;
   181	          })
   182	          .catch((error) => {
   183	            // settleItem never throws, but stay defensive so the pump never wedges.
   184	            results[index] = {
   185	              label: labelFor(items[index], index),
   186	              status: 'rejected',
   187	              error: { name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) },
   188	            };
   189	          })
   190	          .finally(() => {
   191	            active -= 1;
   192	            pump();
   193	          });
   194	      }
   195	    };
   196	
   197	    pump();
   198	  });
   199	}
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   203	 *
   204	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   205	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   206	 */
   207	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   208	  const total = results.length;
   209	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   210	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   211	  return {
   212	    results,
   213	    summary: {
   214	      total,
   215	      succeeded,
   216	      failed,
   217	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   218	    },
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	// 4. STATUS LEDGER
   224	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   228	 *
   229	 * Generalizes the packet-122 `orchestration-status.log`: one line per lineage
   230	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   231	 *
   232	 * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
   233	 * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
   234	 */
   235	function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
   236	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
   237	  fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
   238	}
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
   242	 *
   243	 * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
   244	 * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
   245	 *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
   246	 */
   247	function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
   248	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
   249	  fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
   250	}
   251	
   252	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253	// 5. EXPORTS
   254	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   255	
   256	module.exports = {
   257	  runCappedPool,
   258	  settleItem,
   259	  buildPoolSummary,
   260	  appendStatusLedger,

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts | sed -n '260,430p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	/**
   261	 * Resolve an executor configuration by merging CLI and file sources.
   262	 *
   263	 * @param sources - CLI and file partial configs (CLI takes precedence).
   264	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   265	 */
   266	export function resolveExecutorConfig(sources: {
   267	  cli?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   268	  file?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   269	}): ExecutorConfig {
   270	  const merged = {
   271	    ...(sources.file ?? {}),
   272	    ...(sources.cli ?? {}),
   273	  };
   274	  return parseExecutorConfig(merged);
   275	}
   276	
   277	// ───── FAN-OUT (MULTI-EXECUTOR) CONFIG ─────
   278	//
   279	// Opt-in layer ABOVE the single-executor path. When a fan-out config is present,
   280	// the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
   281	// existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
   282	// path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
   283	// default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
   284	
   285	const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
   289	 *
   290	 * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
   291	 * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
   292	 * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
   293	 */
   294	export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
   295	  label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
   296	    message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
   297	  }),
   298	  count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
   299	  iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
   300	});
   301	
   302	export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
   303	
   304	export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
   305	  executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
   306	  concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
   307	});
   308	
   309	export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
   310	
   311	/**
   312	 * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
   313	 *
   314	 * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
   315	 * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, gemini/devin model
   316	 * whitelists, per-kind flag support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
   317	 * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
   318	 *
   319	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   320	 * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
   321	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   322	 */
   323	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
   324	  const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
   325	  if (!parsed.success) {
   326	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  const config = parsed.data;
   330	
   331	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
   332	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   333	    const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, ...executorSubset } = entry;
   334	    try {
   335	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
   336	    } catch (err) {
   337	      if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
   338	        throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   339	          issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
   340	        });
   341	      }
   342	      throw err;
   343	    }
   344	  });
   345	
   346	  // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
   347	  const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
   348	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   349	    if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
   350	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   351	        issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
   352	      });
   353	    }
   354	    seenLabels.add(entry.label);
   355	  });
   356	
   357	  // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
   358	  const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
   359	  for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
   360	    if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
   361	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   362	        issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
   363	      });
   364	    }
   365	    expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
   366	  }
   367	
   368	  return config;
   369	}
   370	
   371	/**
   372	 * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
   373	 *
   374	 * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
   375	 * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
   376	 * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
   377	 *
   378	 * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
   379	 * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
   380	 */
   381	export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
   382	  const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
   383	  for (const entry of config.executors) {
   384	    if (entry.count === 1) {
   385	      lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
   386	      continue;
   387	    }
   388	    for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
   389	      lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
   390	    }
   391	  }
   392	  return lineages;
   393	}
