     1	import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
     2	import { mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs';
     3	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
     4	import { join } from 'node:path';
     5	
     6	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     7	
     8	const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
     9	const {
    10	  runCappedPool,
    11	  appendStatusLedger,
    12	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
    13	} = require('../../scripts/fanout-pool.cjs') as {
    14	  runCappedPool: (options: {
    15	    items: Array<{ label?: string } | unknown>;
    16	    concurrency: number;
    17	    worker: (item: unknown, ctx: { index: number }) => Promise<unknown>;
    18	    onEvent?: (event: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
    19	  }) => Promise<{
    20	    results: Array<{ label: string; status: string; output?: unknown; error?: { message: string } }>;
    21	    summary: { total: number; succeeded: number; failed: number; all_failed: boolean };
    22	  }>;
    23	  appendStatusLedger: (ledgerPath: string, entry: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
    24	  writeOrchestrationSummary: (summaryPath: string, summary: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
    25	};
    26	
    27	// Flush the microtask queue past any number of chained .then/.finally hops by
    28	// yielding to a macrotask — deterministic regardless of how many ticks the
    29	// pool's admission chain (.then -> .finally -> pump) takes.
    30	const flush = () => new Promise<void>((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 0));
    31	
    32	// A controllable worker: each item resolves only when its gate is released.
    33	// `entered` is append-only (worker invocation order); `inFlight` reflects the
    34	// live set (gate deleted on release) so the cap can be asserted precisely.
    35	function makeGatedWorker() {
    36	  const gates = new Map<string, () => void>();
    37	  const entered: string[] = [];
    38	  let active = 0;
    39	  let maxActive = 0;
    40	  const worker = (item: { label: string }) =>
    41	    new Promise<{ label: string }>((resolve) => {
    42	      entered.push(item.label);
    43	      active += 1;
    44	      maxActive = Math.max(maxActive, active);
    45	      gates.set(item.label, () => {
    46	        if (!gates.has(item.label)) return;
    47	        gates.delete(item.label);
    48	        active -= 1;
    49	        resolve({ label: item.label });
    50	      });
    51	    });
    52	  return {
    53	    worker,
    54	    release: (label: string) => gates.get(label)?.(),
    55	    releaseAll: () => [...gates.values()].forEach((fn) => fn()),
    56	    inFlight: () => [...gates.keys()].sort(),
    57	    entered: () => [...entered],
    58	    maxActive: () => maxActive,
    59	  };
    60	}
    61	
    62	const tmpDirs: string[] = [];
    63	function freshTmpDir(): string {
    64	  const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'fanout-pool-'));
    65	  tmpDirs.push(dir);
    66	  return dir;
    67	}
    68	
    69	afterEach(() => {
    70	  while (tmpDirs.length > 0) {
    71	    const dir = tmpDirs.pop();
    72	    if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    73	  }
    74	});
    75	
    76	describe('runCappedPool', () => {
    77	  it('never exceeds the concurrency cap', async () => {
    78	    const gated = makeGatedWorker();
    79	    const items = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e'].map((label) => ({ label }));
    80	    const run = runCappedPool({ items, concurrency: 2, worker: gated.worker });
    81	
    82	    // With cap 2, only the first two items are admitted initially.
    83	    await flush();
    84	    expect(gated.inFlight()).toEqual(['a', 'b']);
    85	    expect(gated.entered()).toEqual(['a', 'b']);
    86	    expect(gated.maxActive()).toBe(2);
    87	
    88	    // Releasing one frees exactly one slot, admitting the next item ('c').
    89	    gated.release('a');
    90	    await flush();
    91	    expect(gated.inFlight()).toEqual(['b', 'c']);
    92	    expect(gated.entered()).toEqual(['a', 'b', 'c']);
    93	
    94	    // Drain the rest, re-releasing until the pool fully settles.
    95	    for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i += 1) {
    96	      gated.releaseAll();
    97	      await flush();
    98	    }
    99	    const result = await run;
   100	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 5, succeeded: 5, failed: 0, all_failed: false });
   101	    expect(gated.entered()).toEqual(['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e']);
   102	    expect(gated.maxActive()).toBe(2);
   103	  });
   104	
   105	  it('preserves input order in results regardless of completion order', async () => {
   106	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   107	      items: [{ label: 'slow' }, { label: 'fast' }],
   108	      concurrency: 2,
   109	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => {
   110	        if (item.label === 'slow') {
   111	          await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 15));
   112	        }
   113	        return { label: item.label };
   114	      },
   115	    });
   116	    expect(result.results.map((r) => r.label)).toEqual(['slow', 'fast']);
   117	    expect(result.results.every((r) => r.status === 'fulfilled')).toBe(true);
   118	  });
   119	
   120	  it('isolates a single failing item without sinking the pool', async () => {
   121	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   122	      items: [{ label: 'ok' }, { label: 'boom' }],
   123	      concurrency: 2,
   124	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => {
   125	        if (item.label === 'boom') throw new Error('lineage executor failed');
   126	        return { ok: true };
   127	      },
   128	    });
   129	    expect(result.results.map((r) => r.status)).toEqual(['fulfilled', 'rejected']);
   130	    expect(result.results[1].error?.message).toBe('lineage executor failed');
   131	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 2, succeeded: 1, failed: 1, all_failed: false });
   132	  });
   133	
   134	  it('marks all_failed=true when every item throws', async () => {
   135	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   136	      items: [{ label: 'a' }, { label: 'b' }, { label: 'c' }],
   137	      concurrency: 2,
   138	      worker: async () => {
   139	        throw new Error('all lineages fail');
   140	      },
   141	    });
   142	    expect(result.results.every((r) => r.status === 'rejected')).toBe(true);
   143	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 3, succeeded: 0, failed: 3, all_failed: true });
   144	  });
   145	
   146	  it('resolves an empty pool with a zero summary (all_failed false)', async () => {
   147	    const result = await runCappedPool({ items: [], concurrency: 2, worker: async () => ({}) });
   148	    expect(result.results).toEqual([]);
   149	    expect(result.summary).toEqual({ total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false });
   150	  });
   151	
   152	  it('clamps a concurrency below 1 up to 1 and still completes', async () => {
   153	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   154	      items: [{ label: 'a' }, { label: 'b' }],
   155	      concurrency: 0,
   156	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => ({ label: item.label }),
   157	    });
   158	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 2, succeeded: 2 });
   159	  });
   160	
   161	  it('emits started + completed/failed ledger events per item', async () => {
   162	    const events: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];
   163	    await runCappedPool({
   164	      items: [{ label: 'ok' }, { label: 'boom' }],
   165	      concurrency: 2,
   166	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => {
   167	        if (item.label === 'boom') throw new Error('x');
   168	        return {};
   169	      },
   170	      onEvent: (event) => events.push(event),
   171	    });
   172	    const byLabel = (label: string) => events.filter((e) => e.label === label).map((e) => e.event);
   173	    expect(byLabel('ok')).toEqual(['started', 'completed']);
   174	    expect(byLabel('boom')).toEqual(['started', 'failed']);
   175	  });
   176	
   177	  it('throws on invalid items or worker', () => {
   178	    expect(() => runCappedPool({ items: 'nope' as unknown as [], concurrency: 1, worker: async () => ({}) })).toThrow(
   179	      TypeError,
   180	    );
   181	    expect(() =>
   182	      runCappedPool({ items: [], concurrency: 1, worker: undefined as unknown as () => Promise<unknown> }),
   183	    ).toThrow(TypeError);
   184	  });
   185	});
   186	
   187	describe('status ledger helpers', () => {
   188	  it('appends JSONL entries to the status ledger', () => {
   189	    const dir = freshTmpDir();
   190	    const ledger = join(dir, 'nested', 'orchestration-status.log');
   191	    appendStatusLedger(ledger, { event: 'started', label: 'minimax-1' });
   192	    appendStatusLedger(ledger, { event: 'completed', label: 'minimax-1', duration_ms: 12 });
   193	    const lines = readFileSync(ledger, 'utf8').trim().split('\n');
   194	    expect(lines).toHaveLength(2);
   195	    expect(JSON.parse(lines[0])).toMatchObject({ event: 'started', label: 'minimax-1' });
   196	    expect(JSON.parse(lines[1])).toMatchObject({ event: 'completed', duration_ms: 12 });
   197	  });
   198	
   199	  it('writes the orchestration summary as pretty JSON', () => {
   200	    const dir = freshTmpDir();
   201	    const summaryPath = join(dir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   202	    writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, { total: 4, succeeded: 3, failed: 1, salvaged: 1 });
   203	    const parsed = JSON.parse(readFileSync(summaryPath, 'utf8'));
   204	    expect(parsed).toEqual({ total: 4, succeeded: 3, failed: 1, salvaged: 1 });
   205	  });
   206	});

codex
## Focus
docs-vs-code drift: command-driven fan-out, deferred intra-lineage waves, and fanout runtime behavior.
## Findings
P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, issue: The docs advertise CLI fan-out as parallel/capped (for example .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:362), but the runner calls blocking `spawnSync` inside the pool worker, so the event loop cannot admit multiple subprocesses concurrently. Fix: replace `spawnSync` with async `spawn`/`execFile` promise handling, or change the docs to describe CLI fan-out as sequential.

P2, .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:158, issue: This still says validation fails for "reserved-but-unwired executor kinds"; .opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:172 mirrors it, but `cli-gemini` and `cli-claude-code` are wired in executor-config and fanout-run. Fix: remove the reserved/unwired wording and describe the current per-executor validation rules.

P2, .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:833, issue: The comment points to a stale `spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml` file for "wave steps", while the live auto YAML keeps wave orchestration only in `reference_only_appendix` and excludes it from workflow steps. Fix: update the comment to point to `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` reference-only appendix and state that intra-lineage wave orchestration is deferred.

P2, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:79, issue: The resource domain table still says `scripts/*.cjs` has only four entry points, even though the same file documents the fanout scripts at lines 165-168 and the files exist. Fix: update the resource-domain and routing text to include `fanout-pool`, `fanout-run`, `fanout-salvage`, and `fanout-merge`.
## Verdict
Command-driven fan-out is documented, but the CLI path is not actually parallel and a few docs still carry stale executor/wave/runtime-surface wording.
Review verdict: CONDITIONAL

## Focus
docs-vs-code drift: command-driven fan-out, deferred intra-lineage waves, and fanout runtime behavior.
## Findings
P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, issue: The docs advertise CLI fan-out as parallel/capped (for example .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:362), but the runner calls blocking `spawnSync` inside the pool worker, so the event loop cannot admit multiple subprocesses concurrently. Fix: replace `spawnSync` with async `spawn`/`execFile` promise handling, or change the docs to describe CLI fan-out as sequential.

P2, .opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:158, issue: This still says validation fails for "reserved-but-unwired executor kinds"; .opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:172 mirrors it, but `cli-gemini` and `cli-claude-code` are wired in executor-config and fanout-run. Fix: remove the reserved/unwired wording and describe the current per-executor validation rules.

P2, .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml:833, issue: The comment points to a stale `spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml` file for "wave steps", while the live auto YAML keeps wave orchestration only in `reference_only_appendix` and excludes it from workflow steps. Fix: update the comment to point to `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` reference-only appendix and state that intra-lineage wave orchestration is deferred.

P2, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:79, issue: The resource domain table still says `scripts/*.cjs` has only four entry points, even though the same file documents the fanout scripts at lines 165-168 and the files exist. Fix: update the resource-domain and routing text to include `fanout-pool`, `fanout-run`, `fanout-salvage`, and `fanout-merge`.
## Verdict
Command-driven fan-out is documented, but the CLI path is not actually parallel and a few docs still carry stale executor/wave/runtime-surface wording.

[{"id":"f-p123-i11-01","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs","line":341,"issue":"The docs advertise CLI fan-out as parallel/capped, but the runner calls blocking spawnSync inside the pool worker, so the event loop cannot admit multiple subprocesses concurrently.","fix":"Replace spawnSync with async spawn/execFile promise handling, or change the docs to describe CLI fan-out as sequential."},{"id":"f-p123-i11-02","severity":"P2","file":".opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md","line":158,"issue":"The research and review command docs still say validation fails for reserved-but-unwired executor kinds, but cli-gemini and cli-claude-code are wired in executor-config and fanout-run.","fix":"Remove the reserved/unwired wording and describe the current per-executor validation rules."},{"id":"f-p123-i11-03","severity":"P2","file":".opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml","line":833,"issue":"The comment points to a stale spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml file for wave steps, while the live auto YAML keeps wave orchestration reference-only and excluded from workflow steps.","fix":"Point to deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml reference-only appendix and state that intra-lineage wave orchestration is deferred."},{"id":"f-p123-i11-04","severity":"P2","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","line":79,"issue":"The resource domain table still says scripts/*.cjs has only four entry points, despite the shipped fanout scripts documented later and present on disk.","fix":"Update the resource-domain and routing text to include fanout-pool, fanout-run, fanout-salvage, and fanout-merge."}]
